dialog.stepLabel: Blurry and Gentle Female Voice: "...Looks like drowning... Strange......"

items.speaker: Blurry and Gentle Female Voice
items.text: ...Looks like drowning... Strange...

items.speaker: Blurry and Gentle Female Voice
items.text: ...No visible signs of trauma. Breathing seems... normal. Heart rate... should be fast... I think.

items.speaker: Gentle Female Voice
items.text: ...You're awake.

items.speaker: Joyful Female Voice
items.text: Let me see! Let me see!

items.speaker: Joyful Female Voice
items.text: Thank goodness you're awake! Or I'll have to try my last-ditch move—Emergency Skills for Junior Patrollers, Lesson One: CPR!

items.speaker: Joyful Female Voice
items.text: Erm, never did it on any living person though.

items.speaker: Gentle Female Voice
items.text: ...Chixia.

items.speaker: Joyful Female Voice
items.text: Sorry, sorry. You just woke up, and here I am, chattin' up a storm...

items.speaker: Gentle Female Voice
items.text: Are you alright? Do you feel any discomfort?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: You can call me Chixia... And, she's Yangyang!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: We are in the Gorges of Spirits. It's on the outskirts of Jinzhou, Huanglong.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Your attire doesn't seem local. Are you a roving traveler from afar, or... someone returning home?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: So you don't know why you're here, where you're from, or who you are...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Baizhi didn't tell us you'd have these aftereffects when you wake up.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The wind has brought us a warning. The Etheric Sea is converging. It seems a new Tacet Field is taking shape.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: That means it's probably gonna get dangerous here soon.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Ugh, I knew it. Our <ano=Terminals>gourds</ano> are out of service because of the <color=Highlight>Etheric Sea's</color> influence. We can't send or receive messages for help now. We better hurry over to meet up with Baizhi at our gathering spot and get outta here.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Guess you really can't recall a thing, but don't worry!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: If you got any questions, we can talk as we move. Yangyang and I will give you the full scoop, every little detail, from the get-go!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Rover... can we call you that before you can remember your true name?

options.text: You can call me that for now.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: We shouldn't stay here. If you don't have other plans, how about leaving with us for now?

options.text: Is there only one way out of the gorge?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh, that's great! Let's go together!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The terrain is complicated here, and we usually take that one route. If you don't mind, why don't we travel together for now? You can decide your future plans once we leave the gorge.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yeah, let's get going!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Once we're outta the gorge, there's only a short walk to reach Jinzhou!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Jinzhou is the safest place round here. The Resonance Nexus provides coverage for the whole city. Besides, we've got a protective barrier and strong fortifications!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hehe, right?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hehe, maybe it's not as big as some other cities. BUT! Jinzhou is always the best place in my heart!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Jinzhou sits between the Desorock Highland and the Norfall Barrens. It's a border city and a fortress. It's very crucial to the safety of Huanglong.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Huh? We've agreed to meet up with Baizhi here... Where is she?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: This is the center of the Etheric Sea.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: ...A Tacet Field?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: ...When did it show up?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: It wasn't here when we just got here.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: But I didn't sense a hint of a Tacet Field's presence...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: So it appeared while we weren't looking? Like, in the blink of an eye?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Or maybe the Etheric Sea can tell where we are? Could it be following us?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The center of this Tacet Field...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Isn't that Baizhi?!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Baizhi!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: BAIZHI!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: We are too far away. Perhaps she can't hear us...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Freshly formed Tacet Fields have a Dormant period. During this time, it is relatively safe.

options.text: You mean this Tacet Field is relatively "safe" now?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The Dormant period of a Tacet Field varies based on its formation time. Generally, the shorter the formation time, the longer the Dormant period. However, with a longer Dormant period, Tacet Discords born within said Tacet Field are usually stronger.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: My apologies. I should have said it is "temporarily" safe.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The Dormant period of a Tacet Field varies based on its formation time. Generally, the shorter the formation time, the longer the Dormant period However, with a longer Dormant period, the Tacet Discords born within are usually stronger.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: We agreed to meet up 'round here, and she said she was off to collect some samples. Gosh, did she really go straight into the heart of a Tacet Field for that?!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Baizhi wouldn't take such risks without careful consideration. Freshly formed Tacet Fields goes through a Dormant period.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The faster a Tacet Field forms, the longer its Dormant period lasts. For now, this Tacet Field should be safe.

options.text: Hopefully we won't bump into Tacet Discords as soon as we get there.
Is there any chance that Baizhi down there is bait?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Accordin' to the <ano=Popular Shows in Jinzhou>Hero Plays</ano>, yes! There's a chance for that!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: True, who could've set the bait, then?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh, I see... the one who seems simple-minded in the group is actually the mastermind with schemes. That's another popular trope!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: No, wait... I'm not the mastermind!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: ...Rover.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh, I see... the one who seems gentle and quiet is actually the villain, hiding right under the protagonist's nose. That's a common trope, too!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: No, wait... Yangyang isn't the villain!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Well, that's not impossible, though...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: What?!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Some Tacet Discords can parrot human speech and behavior, namely the Whisperins. I doubt whether they can imitate a real person to this extent, though...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Right. The Glider can't carry us beyond the Tacet Field's range. Besides, Baizhi is there. We'll have to go down and see what is going on.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yangyang! Rover!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Are you alright? Are you hurt?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Ugh thank goodness! We could see that darn barrier, but couldn't lay a finger on it!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I'm fine too.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I have never seen such a formidable Tacet Discord before, but Rover was able to completely defeat it.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I... didn't really do much. It was all thanks to you, Rover.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Rover, what was that last move called? That looked as cool as Fiamma's killer move!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Aww c'mon, stop being modest! So you didn't even go all out earlier. I saw how you took down that fella with both of my eyes!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: You made a couple kicks and that thing vanished into thin air!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: That's strange. This place isn't known to be so dangerous...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Anyway, come check this out!

options.text: An illusion... left by the Tacet Discord?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Oh, this is... an Echo.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Wow! An Echo of the Elite Class... Someone got lucky today!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: My apologies. We got a little carried away. Does "Reverberation" or "Echo" ring a bell to you, Rover?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Everything in our world is made up of frequencies, and every sound generates a reverberation.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Those "Echoes" are the lingering Reverberations of Tacet Discords after they disintegrate. We can absorb them into the Data Banks in our Pangu Terminals.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh, by Pangu Terminal, she means the gourd-shaped device every Resonator has, developed by researchers in Huanglong!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: But you see, it's not always we get to find these. Also, there's a limit to what your gourd can absorb.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Absorption is only possible when your Data Bank's level is equal to or higher than the Tacet Discord's level. The Terminal can then convert its Reverberation into an "Echo" for use in battle.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: My Terminal issued a warning when it detected that Tacet Discord earlier. It should at least be an Overlord Class.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Rover, would you mind checking your Data Bank level?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: No need for that! Rover, why don't you just try absorbing it with your Terminal?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: ...Oh. So it doesn't work.

options.text: What will happen if I can't absorb it?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: No worries! You can start with smaller ones before tackling this guy here.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: There should be some sweet Baby Roseshrooms 'round here. Let's go check those out, shall we?

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: It won't take long before the Reverberations disappear and the Etheric Sea vanishes. Once that happens, the Resonance Beacon signals will come back.

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: The Tacet Field will dissipate, biding time for its next resurgence.

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: We'll need to report the unusual phenomena here to the City Hall and the Academy.

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: Let's make our exit.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Baizhi!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Baizhi! We're in a Dormant Tacet Field, right? Why did you come here by yourself?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Baizhi, you know, we... huh?

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: You are alright, it seems.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: You know, {Male=he;Female=she} was super energetic! Handled a super big TD! Way more spirited than me!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I've been keeping an eye on {Male=his;Female=her} condition through the Streams...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: What... What kinda power is that!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Rover, you...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: You been playin' the fool all along?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I've never seen a Resonance Ability quite like this. You got any other tricks up your sleeve? Like, eating a whole Tacet Discord? Grilling Echoes alive?

options.text: I have no idea what happened.
It's not that different from eating it alive.
How does that relate to my cooking skills...?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Wasn't it your Resonance Ability?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Could it be related to your lost memories? Can you recall anything now?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Indeed. So what would a Tacet Discord taste like? Spicy? Sweet?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I heard some folks tried to cook Gulpuffs, and they even made it a local speciality, didn't they?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Speaking of which, Gulpuff's a type of Tacet Discord, too!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I'm even more curious now... Anyway, spicy food is always the best!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Rover, are you feeling alright? Any discomfort?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Could it be the side effect of fighting that Tacet Discord? Baizhi, can we take Rover to do a check-up at Huaxu Academy's Resonance Medical Department...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Baizhi?

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: Absorbing Echoes with the human body is not entirely unprecedented.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: With the human body...?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I remember Comprehensive Mirror for Historians mentioned this in the Huanglong section's opening...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: “... A celestial being descended, commanding the <ano=universe>Qiankun's</ano> boundless power. Assuming mortal form, they condensed the primordial essence, creating Pangu. Cleaving heaven and earth apart, they aligned the celestial and terrestrial. Their radiance encompassed the four corners. Thus began the illustrious annals of Huanglong, as provinces were first demarcated..."

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: There was a very strong person way back. Like they held the whole universe right in their hands. As there was no Terminal in those days, they absorbed the Tacet Discords with their bare hands!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: and the TD they absorbed was the most powerful of all. That person turned it into the first Echo ever.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Then they used the power of that Echo to rebuild the world, sort out the laws, and that's how the history of Huanglong began...

options.text: Chixia, you must know a lot about history.
What if I am that person...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Actually... I'm not, I hope I was though. That was just some old tales my parents used to tell me, kinda like the Sentinel's Appointment thing.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yangyang's got an impressive memory for the ancient texts. Me? I just happen to roughly remember what the story's about.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Please, Yangyang, stop reciting that! I'm beggin' you!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh my, it's takin' me back to my school days, when I had to recite pages and pages of them... Anyway, that's just a made-up legend, isn't it?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Come on, that was eons ago. Us Resonators do live a little longer, but we're still looking at 'bout a hundred years or so. And the known history of Huanglong stretches back at least a thousand years...

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: If I may say, Rover, you seem quite "mysterious", too.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: That's where the names "Pangu Terminal" and "Qiankun Data Bank" come from.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Whether it's true or not, that story happened a long time ago when Jinzhou didn't even exist.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Could it be that Rover's like, the great-great-great-great-great-{Male=granddaddy;Female=granny} of us all?

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: Let's not jump to conclusions without enough data and proper analysis.

items.speaker: Baizhi
items.text: Rover.
